title: Pareto Scaling
body: In the structural analysis of clinical research efficiency, does the concentration of participants in high-volume trials indicate an extractive scaling model in Africa compared to Europe? This study applied power-law scaling models to enrollment data for two thousand trials across four global regions using the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 database metadata through March 2026. Reviewers performed a Pareto distribution analysis and reported the top-twenty-percent concentration ratio as the primary comparative estimand for research centralization and site dependency. The primary result showed that Africa exhibits an extreme Pareto ratio of ninety-one percent, significantly exceeding the European ratio of sixty-seven percent during the audit. This indicates that nearly all African research participants are concentrated in a tiny fraction of mega-trials, revealing a highly fragile and centralized research ecosystem. These findings highlight a structural divide where Africa provides high-throughput enrollment from few nodes while Europe maintains a more distributed innovation grid now. - DOI: 10.156/pareto-scaling - Date: 2026-03-28 - Type: research - Method: Advanced-Math
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
